blurb:
Rreal Tacos is one of those places the neighborhood keeps to itself but probably should not. The vibe is loud and fun, the service is attentive, and the food actually lives up to what people say about it. Great for a group dinner or a casual night out when you want good food and a couple of good drinks.

Start with the Esquite before anything else. For drinks, the Pineapple Guava pitcher and the Passion Fruit pitcher are both worth ordering, especially if you are sharing with the table. They are refreshing and well-balanced without being too sweet.

They are open until midnight every night except Sunday, so it is also a solid late-night option if you need one.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Royal Spice on Hammond Drive is the kind of Indian place you end up at when you want a real sit-down meal and not just takeout. The staff is genuinely helpful, especially if you're not sure what to order. They'll walk you through the menu and answer questions without making you feel rushed.

The entrees are the main draw and the appetizers are worth ordering too, not just as an afterthought. It's a good spot for a date night or a dinner out with friends who want something a little different. The atmosphere is calm and low-key with quiet music in the background.

They do a lunch service and dinner service with a break in between on weekdays, so check the hours before you head over.
address: 220 Hammond Dr #302, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you need a reliable breakfast or brunch spot in Sandy Springs, J. Christopher's is the one. It's a daytime-only place, open every day until 2pm, and it's the kind of spot where you can bring the whole family without any stress. The vibe is casual and comfortable, good for a lazy Saturday morning or a quick weekday breakfast before work.

They do classic breakfast all day, think omelets and the kind of egg dishes that actually fill you up. They also run seasonal specials, so there's usually something a little different to try beyond the regular menu. The burgers are worth noting too if you show up closer to lunch.

Just remember they close at 2pm every day, so this is strictly a morning and midday stop.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Los Bravos has been a go-to Mexican spot in this area for a long time, and the food from the kitchen is still the reason people keep coming back. It's a reliable pick for a weeknight dinner or a casual family meal without breaking the bank. They're open seven days a week, which is handy.

The food is the draw here. Long-time regulars say the kitchen is solid and consistent, which matters more than anything when you're feeding a family on a Tuesday night.

One honest heads up: several regulars have noted that the margarita prices have gone up quite a bit lately. The food is still a good deal, but if you're planning to order drinks at the bar, just know the tab can add up faster than you'd expect.
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Sunnyside Pizzeria is one of those places that keeps showing up in conversations for a reason. It's in the Sandy Springs Circle shopping center and does really well for a casual dinner out or an easy Friday night with the family. The rating speaks for itself, over a thousand reviews and still sitting at 4.7.

The Hot Honey Char pizza gets a lot of attention and for good reason. The Hot Honey Lemon Pepper Chicken Wings are also a must-order appetizer, sweet and a little spicy in a way that works. The Caesar Salad is a solid starter if you want something simple done right. People save their wing sauce to dip their pizza crust in, which tells you everything you need to know.

They're closed on Mondays, so plan accordingly.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir Suite 149, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road is a fun find if you've never had a Brazilian-style açaí bowl. You build your own bowl and load it up with whatever toppings you want, which is great for picky eaters or if you just like being in control of what you're eating. The vibe is cozy and the staff is genuinely friendly.

Beyond the açaí, they also have crepes and coxinha, which is a popular Brazilian fried chicken snack. It's the kind of place that works well for an afternoon treat or a light lunch, especially with kids.

They're closed on Tuesdays, so plan accordingly.
address: 2145 Roswell Rd Suite 110, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
If you need a special occasion spot that actually feels special, Ray's on the River is the one. It sits right on the Chattahoochee in Sandy Springs, and the setting alone is worth the trip. Think anniversary dinners, milestone birthdays, or just a night when you want to feel like you're treating yourself. It's not cheap, but it's the kind of place where you don't feel like you overpaid when you leave.

The menu leans into Southern seafood, and that's what most people come for. It's been a go-to for this area for years and has over 11,000 reviews to back it up.

Sunday hours are split, so check before you go. They do lunch through 4pm, then reopen for dinner at 5pm.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road punches way above its weight. The owner is genuinely passionate about what he's doing, and you feel it as soon as you walk in. He'll tell you about how the pho is made and why it's different from what you'd find at most places. It's the kind of experience that makes you want to come back.

If you've never had pho before, this is a great place to try it for the first time. The reviews say it's made the authentic way and that it lives up to the hype. Prices are very reasonable, so it's an easy choice for a quick lunch or a low-key weeknight dinner.

They're open every day from 10am to 9pm, which makes it one of the more flexible options in the area.
address: 5980 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you're on the Roswell Road corridor and want a solid pizza without spending a lot, JJ's is the move. It's a no-frills spot that's been putting out good pies for a while now, and the prices are still pretty reasonable. Good for a casual family dinner or just grabbing a pie on the way home.

The stuffed pizza is the thing to get if you haven't tried it yet. Reviewers say one slice is genuinely a full meal, and they're not exaggerating. It's big, filling, and worth the wait if you order delivery.

Just a heads up: they're closed on Tuesdays. And on Sundays they don't open until noon.
address: 2211 Roswell Rd suite 116, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Heirloom Market is one of those places you drive past and almost overlook, but once you go you'll wonder why you waited so long. It's a tiny spot over off Akers Mill with one communal table and a menu that mixes classic BBQ with a Korean-inspired twist. It's the kind of place that has a real following for good reason.

The BBQ is the main event, and the Korean flavors set it apart from your standard smoked meat joint. It's a great lunch stop or early dinner if you're in the Cumberland area during the week.

They're only open Tuesday through Saturday, 11am to 8pm, and they're closed Sunday and Monday. It also gets busy, so don't be surprised if you have to wait a bit.
address: 2243 Akers Mill Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA
